A glob-based check would be safer but more complex; literal strings are auditable and obvious. 2) The AST scan in test_pi_agent_god_mode_entry_is_interactive_only walks up 15 lines for context — a more rigorous version would build the actual AST parent chain and assert the enclosing If statement's test condition matches the interactive trigger. Acceptable today because the only place self.mode='god' lives is the one handler; bigger codebase would warrant the AST traversal. 3) Add a second AST test that fails if pi_agent.py grows another `self.mode = ...` assignment without an interactive trigger guard.
not_done: Manual smoke: running `python scripts/sprint.py --dry-run` interactively to confirm the [sprint] excluding log line fires would be a stronger demonstration. The unit test suite covers all three defence layers but doesn't exercise the full sprint loop end-to-end. Acceptable — sprint is a 700-line script and a true e2e test would need a real ticket, real Claude call, real verify run.

problem: Vision tool (analyze_image / analyze_video) had a hardcoded two-step fallback: prefer Gemini if key present, else Claude. Gemini 429 (quota exhausted) leaked the raw error string into Telegram replies. No circuit breaker meant repeated 429s in a tight loop. Same pattern would have been re-implemented for STT, TTS, etc.
root_cause: Provider selection was hardcoded per-tool with try/except fallback. Each new tool would copy the pattern. Circuit-breaker state would have to be reimplemented per call site. The compression-with-Haiku-fallback (T-092) was the first instance of this pattern; the generalisation was overdue.
countermeasure: Built agent/provider_router.py with ProviderRouter class: walks provider list in order, parses 429 retryDelay (Google format + Anthropic retry-after header), 3 consecutive failures opens a 5-min circuit. All cooldown opens recorded via track_silent so silent_failure_watcher catches them. tools_media.py adds four strict provider wrappers (gemini_flash, gemini_pro, claude_haiku_vision, claude_sonnet_vision) and dispatches analyze_image and analyze_video through a singleton router.
better_future_fix: Provider router pattern should be applied next to: T-124 thinking layer (Groq -> Haiku fallback uses this pattern), T-115 deferred Groq tool_use_failed brownout, future STT/TTS providers. Each is a copy of this T-121 work.
lessons: The third instance of a pattern is when you extract — compression (T-092) was the first, vision (T-121) is the second-and-extract, thinking layer (T-124) will be the third user
retry_after_s=0 must not fall back to default — use "is not None" check, not "or" (truthiness bug found in test)
Provider chains need both explicit RateLimitError class AND implicit detection (message inspection) — different SDKs raise different exception classes for 429
Circuit breaker state must be per-process in-memory; persistence would create resume-on-restart bugs where a cooldown outlives the actual rate limit

problem: Closing a ticket meant manually moving a JSON file. No enforcement of verify.py PASS, no solution-link check, ADRs got forgotten, effort_actual never recorded. The close path was a single point of drift.
root_cause: Discipline depended on humans remembering each step. No tooling encoded the gate. Each missed gate compounded: verify skipped under pressure, solutions not logged, ADRs deferred indefinitely.
countermeasure: scripts/close_ticket.py is now the sanctioned close path. Four gates: verify.py PASS, SOLUTIONS.jsonl entry, ADR file (when adr_required), no_new_debt soft gate. --no-solution and --skip-verify flags for legitimate exceptions; --force bypasses ALL gates with track_silent record. Prompts for effort_actual_minutes; writes to ticket JSON. Idempotent (already-closed exits 0).
better_future_fix: Add a pre-commit hook that warns on manual ticket moves to tickets/closed/ — currently the only enforcement is convention. Also wire retro.py to compute estimate-vs-actual bias once 10+ tickets have effort_actual recorded.
lessons: Workflow discipline that depends on memory drifts; encode it as a script
--force flag with mandatory logging is the right escape hatch — gives flexibility without losing audit trail
Dogfooding the tool to close its own ticket is the cleanest validation

problem: Telegram bot dispatched every message to process_input independently. User sending Hey/Supppp/Hey in 5 seconds got three separate replies. L2 distillation ran on N-message thresholds, fragmenting natural conversational units. There was no notion of a conversational bubble.
root_cause: Pi treated messages as atomic. Conversations are not. Humans send incomplete thoughts then finish them; rapid bursts are one intent. Without a bubble abstraction, every downstream concern (recall, distillation, response cost) compounded the fragmentation.
countermeasure: agent/bubble.py exports BubbleCollector. Telegram text handler enqueues each message; collector waits PI_BUBBLE_IDLE_MS (default 6000ms) of silence per chat_id, then emits a single Bubble{messages, started_at, ended_at, has_media, reply_targets, closed_reason} to a consumer queue. Bubble force-flushes on (a) idle timeout, (b) max_messages, (c) max_bubble_ms, (d) any media message, (e) lifecycle event via collector.flush(chat_id). TelegramTools._dispatch_bubble runs on the consumer thread, joins text with newlines, calls process_input once, sends typing indicator immediately on bubble-open.
better_future_fix: Bubble is currently bound to Telegram only. Voice and future channels should use the same primitive. Also: the consumer thread is single-threaded; if multiple chats produce bubbles simultaneously, they are dispatched serially. For scale this might need a small thread pool, but ordering must be preserved per chat_id.
lessons: A unit of conversation is a primitive that touches every downstream system (recall, distillation, response cost) — defining it first unblocks the rest of Phase 8.8
Per-chat_id locks plus a shared consumer queue is the right concurrency shape — no global serialisation, no per-chat thread explosion
Force-flush API is the right escape hatch for lifecycle commands (/exit, /clear) — they need synchronous access to the open bubble
use_bubble=False default-on is the right migration strategy; tests can opt out without changing production behaviour

problem: Fixes and decisions discussed in chat regularly failed to get ticketed. This session alone produced 5+ after-the-fact tickets that lived in conversation for hours before being written down. ticket_candidate_miner (Skill 8) scanned code+logs but not the conversation transcript itself.
root_cause: Conversation is a first-class source of engineering signal but the passive skill suite ignored it. The longer a session, the more decisions got lost in chat history that never became tracked work.
countermeasure: scripts/passive/conversation_ticket_miner.py (Skill 14) reads last 24h of logs/turns.jsonl via _tail_jsonl, asks Groq llama-3.3-70b to extract fixes/decisions/recurring complaints, dedups against existing ticket titles via cheap word-overlap proxy (threshold 0.7), writes fresh candidates to analysis/conversation_candidates.jsonl. PASS=0, WARN=1-3, FAIL=4+. Wired into passive_daily_digest as Skill 14.
better_future_fix: Word-overlap dedup is naive — a real embedding-based dedup (cosine via sentence-transformers, reusing T-080 machinery) would catch rewordings the word-overlap proxy misses. Also: candidates should auto-promote to tickets after N days of repeated extraction without action.
lessons: Passive skills should not just observe code state — conversation IS state
Cheap proxies (word overlap) are fine for first-pass dedup; embedding cost only justified once false-positive rate hurts
Strict JSON output from LLM needs code-fence stripping defensively — Groq sometimes wraps despite instructions

problem: Pi L3 was append-only. User is 19 sat there forever, even after the next birthday. User born 2006-08-17 and User is 19 coexisted as independent facts with no derivation link.
root_cause: No maintenance loop existed. T-078 added invalid_at as a manual flag; T-080 added embedding dedup as one-off. Neither generalised into a continuous process. Derived facts (age, days-until) need automatic recomputation on a schedule.
countermeasure: agent/caretaker.py exports lite(db_path), backfill(db_path), detect_derivable(content), and a formula registry (age_from_birthday, days_until_date, days_since_date). Schema extended (idempotent migration) with kind, source_id, recompute_after, formula columns. memory_write auto-detects derivable content and spawns paired derived rows. Caretaker runs per-bubble (via TelegramTools._dispatch_bubble) and daily (via retention_tick.py). filelock data/caretaker.lock prevents bubble + cron races. ADR-007 documents the lifecycle skeleton (stages B and C in T-125b/T-125c).
files_changed: tools/tools_memory.py (4-column migration + derived-fact spawn on memory_write)
better_future_fix: Detection is regex-only (born YYYY-MM-DD). Should extend to: "my birthday is X", "X was born", "DOB X", and ISO-8601 variations. Also: derived rows currently dont have a way to delete themselves when their source is invalidated — needs a cascade in T-125b/c.
lessons: Schema migrations must be idempotent — Pi runs the migration block at every startup
Soft mutations only (update content, never DELETE) — keeps audit trail and makes rollback trivial
Filelock is the right primitive when multiple triggers (per-bubble + cron) might race on the same DB
Test time-jumps with explicit now= injection rather than freezegun — cheaper and clearer

problem: When user replied to a previous Pi message in Telegram (quote/tag), Pi could not see the quoted text. It treated the user new reply as standalone. Result: tagged images got re-analysed (wasting vision tokens), tagged text lost its context entirely.
root_cause: Telegram exposed reply_to_message on every reply event but Pi handler discarded it. The bubble layer (T-122) started preserving it in reply_targets but nothing acted on those targets yet.
countermeasure: memory/recall.py exports recall_referenced(text, db_path, threshold=0.85, limit=3) and format_recall_context(hits). Walks L3 (skipping invalidated and derived-pending rows), embeds each candidate via existing memory.semantic_dedup.get_embedding, cosine-compares against query embedding, returns top-N hits above threshold. TelegramTools._dispatch_bubble now calls recall for each entry in bubble.reply_targets, prepends a RECALLED CONTEXT block to the joined text before dispatch.
better_future_fix: For Telegram photo replies, the recall should bypass MediaTools.analyze_image entirely when a hit corresponds to a previous analysis — currently it still re-analyses if a media message arrives. That requires linking media bubbles to their image-analysis L3 entries, deferred to a follow-up ticket.
lessons: Reusing existing infrastructure (memory.semantic_dedup) means new features ship in 2h not 2 days
Skipping derived-pending placeholders in recall search prevents Pi from quoting (pending recompute from xyz) to itself — a subtle bug avoided by filter in the SQL
Best-effort failure (recall returns [] on any error) is the right contract for an enhancement layer — never block the main flow

problem: Inputs like "no sauce in subway aghhh" or "wdym" confused Pi. Pi grabbed the closest L3 hit (which might be a noisy session summary) and answered literally. No deliberation step existed between message arrival and main-model dispatch.
root_cause: The main response model carried two burdens: understand the user AND craft a response. When input was ambiguous or colloquial, both burdens failed together. Splitting comprehension from generation lets a cheap model handle the first burden well.
countermeasure: agent/thinking.py exports normalise(text, history) returning {intent, normalised_query, confidence}. Two-job lite version (recall/clarifier/memory-id deferred to T-129). Provider chain: Groq llama-3.3-70b primary → Claude Haiku 4.5 fallback → silent skip on both-fail. ALWAYS-ON for non-command bubbles. Direct commands (exit/help/mode switches/clear) bypass via _BYPASS_COMMANDS set. TelegramTools._dispatch_bubble runs recall (T-123) and thinking (T-124) in parallel threads after bubble close — combined latency wins over serial.
better_future_fix: Thinking output is prepended as a context block but the main model may ignore it. Need to A/B test whether reply quality actually improves. Also: thinking should cache results when the same bubble fires multiple times in a row (rare but possible during retries).
lessons: JSON parsing from LLM output needs defensive code-fence stripping AND substring extraction — even with explicit JSON-only instructions, models sometimes wrap
Bypass set should be explicit and short, not regex — commands are a small enumeration and easier to audit when literal
Parallel I/O (recall + thinking) is a 2x latency win for almost-free complexity cost — just two threads + join with timeout

problem: Telegram had no lifecycle commands. To end a session cleanly, user had to close the chat or wait for natural session timeout. There was no way to reset context without ending the whole bot.
root_cause: Sessions in Telegram were ambiguous — when did one end? When did context get cleared? Without explicit commands, the user had no agency over their session lifecycle.
countermeasure: /exit and /clear handlers added to tools_telegram.py. Both force-flush the open bubble via BubbleCollector.flush(chat_id) FIRST so the user partial thought is dispatched under the current session_id and not lost. /exit then calls agent.session.on_exit synchronously (runs caretaker + summary). /clear rotates session_id and clears agent.messages + agent.history but leaves memory tier untouched. Help text updated. AFK/back removed per Ash decision.
better_future_fix: On /exit, the goodbye message is sent before on_exit completes — Telegram users see goodbye fast. But on_exit can take 5-10s for full memory sync; an interim status message would be friendly.
lessons: Lifecycle commands MUST force-flush the bubble first; otherwise the user last thought is silently discarded
session_id rotation alone is enough for /clear — touching the memory tier would lose context that should persist
AFK was removed not because it was hard but because the requirement was unclear; cutting scope is the right call when the design has not landed

problem: T-125a covered derived recompute but near-identical L3 entries (User likes coffee / User loves coffee) still accumulated independently. Search noise grew over time; subway-order-class recall confusion got worse with each session.
root_cause: L3 dedup existed as a one-off (T-080) but was never wired as a continuous process. The caretaker pattern from T-125a made the natural home for it.
countermeasure: agent/caretaker.full(db) now runs lite() + an embedding dedup pass. Walks active rows (excluding derived/invalidated/already-superseded), groups by category, computes embeddings via memory.semantic_dedup.get_embedding, marks losers with superseded_by = winner_id when cosine >= 0.92. Merge policy: highest importance wins, tied importance breaks by newer created_at, tied created_at breaks by id ordering. Bounded at 500 rows/run. New superseded_by column added to l3_cache (idempotent migration). All L3 search paths (memory_read, _hybrid_search_l3, _l3_fast_path, _search_l3_cache, get_l3_context, memory/recall) now also exclude superseded_by rows. Wired to session-exit (caretaker_full step, 10s budget, daemon thread) and daily cron (retention_tick.py).
files_changed: tools/tools_memory.py (superseded_by column migration + 6 query filter updates)
scripts/retention_tick.py (full instead of lite in daily cron)
better_future_fix: Cross-category dedup is impossible by design (same-category requirement prevents false merges). But sometimes the SAME fact is written under different categories (note vs profile). Future: cross-category dedup with HIGHER threshold (0.95+) and a category-conflict resolver. Also: when winner is itself later invalidated, losers should NOT be promoted back — they stay superseded.
lessons: Soft mutations (superseded_by, not DELETE) keep an audit trail and let you rollback by clearing the field
Same-category requirement is the cheapest false-merge defense — works without ML
Search-path filter updates are easy to miss; grep for the existing filter (invalid_at IS NULL) and add the new one everywhere it appears
Resumable exit tests should use IN checks, not equality — that future-proofed us against T-125b adding a new step

problem: T-125a + T-125b handled recompute and dedup. Contradicting writes (User lives in Atlanta followed later by User lives in Multan) still coexisted as independent active facts; recall pulled whichever ranked higher, and the latest fact was not authoritative.
root_cause: No automatic mechanism existed to detect and invalidate the loser when contradictory values appeared for the same topic. T-078 invalid_at was manual; T-080 dedup was identity-not-contradiction.
countermeasure: agent/caretaker.py: added scan_contradictions(db) and deep(db). Contradiction scan groups active rows by (category, topic_key) — topic_key extracted via _topic_key (known relation verbs like lives_in, works_at first; fallback to first-2-content-tokens). When a group has multiple distinct values (via _value_tail), newest wins; older rows get invalid_at set. Deep mode is a Haiku-backed nightly pattern review per category — informational only (logged via track_silent.deep_review). full() now runs lite + dedup + contradiction scan inline. deep() wired into retention_tick.py daily cron. ADR-007 finalised with all four lifecycle states (stated/derived/superseded/invalidated), state transitions, mode table, failure modes + recovery.
better_future_fix: Topic-key heuristic uses a fixed list of relation verbs. Misses creative phrasings like "I am now in X" without an explicit verb. Future work: cascade invalidation (when source row invalidated, derived children get invalidated too) and embedding-based topic clustering for the long tail.
lessons: Contradiction detection is a topic-clustering problem not a value-equality problem — same value with different topics is fine, same topic with different values is the conflict
Newest-wins is the right default but can be wrong on typo cases; deep-mode Haiku is the safety net catching those
Soft invalidation throughout means a wrongly-flagged contradiction is one UPDATE statement away from recovery
ADR finalisation belongs in the LAST stage of a multi-stage feature — writing it at stage A would have been premature; writing it now captures what actually shipped

Schema migrations + per-test in-memory schemas need to stay in sync — verify caught a wave of regressions when superseded_by was missing from 4 test files; a shared fixture might prevent this in future

title: Telegram media routes through process_input + plain-text replies (parse_mode=None)
root_cause: Two compounding bugs: (1) handle_photo/video/document called MediaTools directly and replied with raw vision text — image never entered process_input, no L1/L2 write, no consciousness applied, no conversational continuity. (2) Bot constructed with parse_mode=Markdown; raw vision output containing unescaped */_ chars caused Telegram 400 "can't parse entities" on reply, silently swallowed.
fix: Added _send_plain_chunks (parse_mode=None) + _media_route (frames vision/STT output as user_input for process_input). Refactored photo/video/document handlers to: analyze → frame → _process_text → plain reply. Voice handler kept STT+_process_text; only swapped reply path to plain chunks. Vision API failures pass the error string as user_input so Pi acknowledges gracefully instead of going silent.
tests: 6 new tests in test_telegram_media_routing.py + 31 existing telegram/bubble/STT tests still PASS + verify.py PASS (206 syntax-clean, 85 tests, 0 fail)
better_future_fix: Wire the _dispatch_bubble media branch (currently stubbed as "media-only bubble — handler not yet wired") so rapid media + text in same window get bundled like text bubbles. Separately, add observability events (telegram.photo_received) so future regressions are visible in silent_failures.db.

problem: Per-turn cost and token data (total_cost, t_in, t_out, model, duration_s) computed in _respond_via_config but never surfaced to the user. Budget-steering requires visibility.
countermeasure: Added agent/cost_footer.py (pure formatter + env check). format_cost_footer() renders [$cost · tok_in · tok_out · model · duration]. emit_if_enabled() prints to stderr (never into final_text) when PI_SHOW_COST=on. Wired into _respond_via_config step 6 inside cfg.public_logging gate — god mode never emits. Wrapped in try/except per Inv.9 (non-fatal observability).
better_future_fix: When T-131 status line lands, cost_footer can feed into the status bar instead of a raw stderr line — single unified UX for all per-turn metadata.
lessons: Observability helpers belong in their own module so they can be unit-tested without instantiating the agent.
stderr is the correct channel for dev/debug output — never splice cost info into final_text which all downstream consumers see.
public_logging gate already encodes the god-mode privacy invariant — piggyback new observability on it rather than duplicating the check.

problem: Pi shows 3-line startup banner then goes silent — mode, cost, open tickets, L3 depth all invisible after banner scrolls past. Users lose cost awareness mid-session.
countermeasure: Added agent/status_line.py: format_status_line() pure formatter (god mode redacts session_id), emit_if_enabled() reads agent.mode/session_id/turn_count/evolution.get_daily_cost/open ticket glob/L3 COUNT(*) and prints to stderr when PI_STATUS_LINE=on. Wired into pi_agent.py run() loop after print(response). Never fires in Telegram/voice paths. Also fixed 2 pre-existing flaky test suites (test_memory.py + test_integration.py): hardcoded unique tokens triggered word-overlap dedup on second run — replaced with UUID-first content. Fixed UnicodeEncodeError in tools_memory.py print (→ → ->) and added PYTHONUTF8=1 to verify.py subprocess env.
better_future_fix: When T-130 cost footer and T-131 status line both exist, consolidate into a single post-turn metadata line to avoid two separate prints. A unified status bar is cleaner than independent prints.
lessons: L3 dedup strips unique[a-z0-9]{4,} suffixes by design — test markers must be UUID-first, not UUID-suffix.
Word-overlap dedup fires on shared stable words even when the marker differs — 6/7 word overlap exceeds the 0.80 threshold.
Windows cp1252 cannot encode arrow characters in print statements — always use ASCII -> in log prints or set PYTHONUTF8=1 in subprocess env.
Observability hooks that read agent state must be module-level-importable for patch() to work in tests — avoid lazy imports inside functions.

problem: Pi startup feels cold — all session context (last topic, last ticket touched) is available in L3 and fs, but never surfaced at startup.
countermeasure: Added _format_continuation_line(mode) to agent/startup_banner.py: reads most-recent category=session_history row from l3_cache via direct sqlite3 (no MemoryTools import to keep startup fast/import-safe), reads last-touched ticket from tickets/closed/ mtime. Formats "Last session YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM · "summary..." · last touched T-NNN". God mode queries god_memory.db. All reads wrapped in try/except — returns empty on any failure. Also updated test_startup_banner.py: changed len(lines) == 3 to >= 3 since continuation line is now optional.
better_future_fix: Continuation line reads the raw session_history content which is the full session summary (can be verbose). Future: store a dedicated short_summary field alongside the full session_history, or truncate at distillation time.
lessons: Direct sqlite3.connect in startup helpers avoids MemoryTools import chain (~100ms startup overhead) while still reading L3.
Existing banner tests with exact line-count assertions break when new optional lines are added — use >= rather than == for line counts.
god/public db path decision belongs in the formatting helper, passed via mode param already available in format_banner().

problem: L3 memory not user-inspectable — no way to audit or steer Pi memory without raw SQL
countermeasure: Created scripts/memory_cli.py standalone argparse script with 4 subcommands: list (SELECT with WHERE filters, JSON output, full_id flag), forget (word-overlap top-3 candidates → confirm → UPDATE invalid_at), pin (UPDATE importance=10 + pinned=1 if column exists), why (full row metadata trace). --god flag requires PI_GOD_CLI=1 env — either alone refuses. test_memory_cli.py: 17 tests using seeded temp SQLite DB.
better_future_fix: Add thin shim in pi.py so it can be called as "pi memory list" instead of "python scripts/memory_cli.py list".
lessons: Standalone scripts with no PiAgent import are the lowest-blast-radius way to expose DB operations
Both --god flag AND PI_GOD_CLI=1 env required prevents accidental god-mode access from a single typo

problem: Single importance int (1-10) cannot express multi-factor memory salience — a dying grandparent fact and a favorite color fact get the same weight at importance=8
countermeasure: memory/salience.py: composite_salience = 0.30*imp_norm + 0.25*surprise + 0.20*goal_alignment + 0.15*recency + 0.10*affect_bonus. All inputs NULL-safe with neutral defaults. 6 new l3_cache columns via PRAGMA idempotent migration. _l3_fast_path + _hybrid_search_l3 BM25 path both updated with 12-col SELECT + OperationalError fallback (NULL aliases for pre-migration schemas). PI_SALIENCE_MODE=composite env gate. Also fixed _tokenize to treat underscores as word separators ([^a-zA-Z0-9\s]) to fix BM25 matching on UUID-prefixed test content. test_salience.py: 30 tests.
better_future_fix: Surprise score currently defaults to 0.5 neutral (no real cosine computation at write time). Real novelty detection would require one cosine pass against same-category rows — deferred to T-138.
lessons: Pre-migration schema fallback (NULL aliases via OperationalError catch) is essential for any 12+ column SELECT on a shared test DB
Tokenize on underscores as well as punctuation — BM25 needs identifier-friendly tokenization
_tokenize consistency between corpus and query is critical; a corpus token t001_rw_abc123 does not match query token abc123 without underscore splitting

problem: Stale L3 facts never decay — a fact last accessed 6 months ago ranks identically to one accessed yesterday, filling hot context with dead weight
countermeasure: effective_importance = importance * exp(-decay_rate * days_since_access). Per-category decay rates (permanent_profile=0.002/d half-life ~350d; session_history=0.05/d half-life ~14d). pinned=1 rows are immune. DecayArchivePolicy in agent/retention.py: rows with effective_importance < 1.0 have active_until set to now (soft-archive, recoverable). Gated by PI_DECAY_ARCHIVE=on env. test_decay.py: 11 tests.
better_future_fix: Ship PI_DECAY_ARCHIVE behind a 2-week soak flag before enabling archival in prod. Monitor weekly_memory_audit for unexpected mass-archival of useful facts.
lessons: effective_importance computed at query time (never stored) avoids stale snapshots and stays accurate without background jobs
Archive != delete — active_until=now makes rows invisible to default queries but recoverable via --include-archived; never hard-delete prematurely

problem: memory_delete wiped entire L3 (20 entries) on a single call — _l3_fast_path returned all rows regardless of query match (imp+recency non-zero even with match_score=0), and memory_delete had no bulk guard or true soft-delete
countermeasure: (1) _l3_fast_path: added match_score > 0 gate — only content-matching rows returned now. (2) _tokenize: split on underscores ([^a-zA-Z0-9\s]) so UUID-prefixed content tokenizes correctly for BM25. (3) memory_delete: UUID_PREFIX_RE routes id-looking targets to id LIKE query (not content search). Bulk guard: >3 entries requires force=True. True soft-delete: sets invalid_at (recoverable) instead of hard DELETE. WAL snapshot to data/delete_wal.jsonl before every modification. (4) test_l3_search_fast_path.py: updated assertion to verify non-matching rows are NOT returned (was checking they rank lower). test_memory_delete.py: 11 new tests.
better_future_fix: memory_delete should ultimately require ID-based deletion only (no content-match deletion) to eliminate false-positive deletion risk entirely. Content-based deletes should go through memory_cli forget which shows candidates and confirms.
lessons: _l3_fast_path non-match filter: importance + recency scores alone should never cause a row to be returned — there must be a content match. This is true for any search-flavored retrieval.
Soft-delete = invalid_at, never hard DELETE — recoverable is always better for user data
Bulk guards prevent foot-gun operations: more than N entries requires explicit force flag

problem: Groq 400 tool_use_failed triggered 300s brownout — provider healthy, model just generated a bad tool call. Session blocked from Groq for 5 min after one browse attempt.
countermeasure: _is_generation_error(exc) classifier added to LLMRouter. Checks tool_use_failed/context_length_exceeded/content_policy_violation in exception string + status_code==400. Generation errors skip _mark_brownout, log WARNING instead of ERROR. If tools were active when tool_use_failed fires, retries once with tools=[] so model can answer in plain text. Rate-limit 429 and 5xx still brownout as before. 9 new tests.
better_future_fix: Track generation error rate per provider to detect model degradation without punishing the provider unfairly.
lessons: 4xx errors have two different semantics: provider failure (brownout correct) vs model generation failure (brownout wrong). Always classify before penalizing.
A no-tools retry on tool_use_failed is almost always better than 5 min of silence

problem: Awareness shortcut returned Atlanta weather when user asked about Multan; awareness snapshot reproduced verbatim by Groq/Cerebras as response; single-word messages bypassed shortcut incorrectly.
countermeasure: _extract_weather now compares user place hints against cached city — returns None on mismatch so LLM uses tools. awareness snapshot header includes read-only instruction (never output verbatim). try_answer_from_awareness requires >= 2 words to fire. LIVE AWARENESS check changed from exact string to substring so header variant still matches.
better_future_fix: Awareness shortcut should cross-reference the snapshot city against all known user locations and refuse to serve cached data for any unfamiliar city.
lessons: Cached data shortcuts must validate that the cache covers the specific request — returning wrong-city weather is worse than returning nothing
LLMs reproduce system prompt content verbatim when confused; explicit read-only annotations in the prompt help prevent this

problem: Pi claims inability (cant analyze docs) then provides accurate details anyway — erodes trust; also hallucinated image descriptions from context instead of actual vision analysis
countermeasure: consciousness_normie.txt: explicit image rules (CAN analyze, describe only what actually seen, never guess from context), document rules (cannot open files, but ACTIVE CONTEXT exception for already-analyzed content), hard T-141 rule (never refuse then answer — pick one), explicit ACTIVE CONTEXT rule (if the fact is there, use it).
better_future_fix: Add a capability pre-check in normie response path that validates: if model is about to refuse AND the answer exists in ACTIVE CONTEXT, skip the refusal.
lessons: Refuse XOR answer — never both. Dual-message patterns (I cant... *then* here is the answer) are worse than either alone.
Vision hallucination from context is subtle — model latches on to user hints and fabricates plausible descriptions. Explicit prompt instruction to look at the actual image helps.

problem: Pi could not see its own prior replies. extract_text_from_messages and _build_context only captured blocks via hasattr(block,"text") (SDK objects) or tool_result dicts, but _build_assistant_content stores every assistant turn as a {type:text,text:...} dict — so Pi assistant text was dropped from normie session context, root compression input, AND session-summary L2 distillation. Root cause of T-143 incoherence.
countermeasure: Added shared _block_text() helper handling SDK objects + {type:text} dicts + tool_result dicts; routed both extractors through it. Guarded truncate_messages_safely against returning [] (backward-walk fallback then full-list). Added testing/test_context_fidelity.py that stores messages in the REAL production shape (assistant = dict-block list) and asserts replies survive.
better_future_fix: Normie still has only a 5-turn / 300-char string-reconstructed window even with the fix (T-149). Pass a real truncated message array to Groq instead of single_message_ctx, and add the integration coherence harness (T-152) so this class of bug regresses loudly.
lessons: When you change a stored data shape (SDK object -> canonical dict in R8), grep every reader of that data. The extractors were never migrated and the dead .text branch hid it.
Tests that use synthetic plain-string content cannot catch shape-mismatch bugs. Tests must store data in the exact shape production uses.

A green test suite measured plumbing, not the emergent property (conversation coherence). Self-reported tickets (T-143) can ship confident-but-wrong root causes — verify with a repro before trusting the suggested fix.

problem: Normie sent only the current turn (single_message_ctx) + a lossy 300-char session_ctx string, so it had ~5-turn goldfish memory even after T-148.
countermeasure: Added ModeConfig.ctx_message_window; normie now sends a real bounded 16-message array (both sides) via truncate_messages_safely. Disabled session_ctx_inject for normie (messages carry context). OpenAI-compat providers flatten dict content via anthropic_messages_to_openai.
better_future_fix: Tune window vs Groq TPD budget empirically; consider per-mode window sizing.
lessons: Cost optimizations (single_message_ctx) can silently destroy coherence — measure against the user-facing property, not just token count.

problem: Root history compression was lossy-on-lossy: 400-char per-message clip + flat 300-token '3-5 bullet' summary crushed whole sessions.
countermeasure: Raised clip to 1200 (_CTX_CLIP); summary budget scales with input (_summary_budget: 60 tok/msg, 300-1024 clamp); prompt rewritten to preserve decisions/paths/identifiers/numbers verbatim.
better_future_fix: Rolling summary-of-summary so old context degrades gracefully instead of a cliff at message 30.
lessons: Cost-driven caps that aren't sized to input silently degrade output; scale budgets with load.

problem: _prefetch_memory queried only keywords[0] via single-keyword lexical read; multi-concept questions missed.
countermeasure: Semantic search first (memory_search_semantic on full keyword phrase), multi-keyword merged+deduped lexical fallback when semantic returns [] (no key/embeddings/private ns).
better_future_fix: Weight merged hits by score; cap prompt budget.
lessons: When you ship a better retriever (semantic), migrate the latency-sensitive paths to it — prefetch was left on single-keyword BM25.

problem: Self-report tickets carried authoritative-but-wrong fixes (T-143); sprint runner could auto-implement a wrong diagnosis + green verify = falsely closed bug.
countermeasure: sprint.ticket_confidence() classifies tickets; pick_ticket auto path runs only 'verified' tickets (self-report defaults to hypothesis), forced_id bypasses. create_ticket stamps root_cause_confidence=hypothesis.
better_future_fix: Promote hypothesis->verified automatically when a linked reproducing test is attached.
lessons: An autonomous system must distinguish its own guesses from facts before acting on them.

problem: privacy_publish_guard flagged api_key=CEREBRAS_API_KEY (a variable ref) as a credential leak, FAILing every commit (cry-wolf).
countermeasure: Credential check now flags only quoted literals or known key shapes (_is_real_secret); bare identifiers / env lookups / placeholders pass. Shape patterns (sk-/Bearer/JWT) unchanged.
better_future_fix: Make the guard repo-visibility aware (impl-tracked is fine in a private repo) — see T-155.
lessons: A privacy gate that cries wolf gets ignored; a FAIL must always mean a real secret.

problem: Pi replies lacked semantic coherence.
countermeasure: Root-caused to T-148 (assistant turns dropped) + T-149 (normie no real message array); both fixed, T-152 harness proves coherence.
better_future_fix: Per-conversation L3 scoping (T-142).
lessons: Verify self-reported root causes; the original T-143 fix was wrong.

problem: privacy guard FAILed on tracked impl — wrong now repo is private.
countermeasure: repo_is_private() via env/git-config; check_private_impl PASSes-with-note when private, FAILs when public.
better_future_fix: Auto-detect visibility via GitHub API.
lessons: A guard's threat model must track reality.

problem: Context-cued recall — boost retrieval for same-mode and same-scope matches (encoding-specificity principle)
countermeasure: Context-cued recall: same-mode (0.15) + same-scope (0.20) retrieval boosts via _context_boosts, both retrieval paths, behind PI_CONTEXT_CUED_RECALL (default off). Added l3_cache.mode/scope columns + current_scope field (the dependency). Composes in one query.
files_changed: tools/tools_memory.py — memory_read accepts current_mode + current_scope; _hybrid_search_l3 applies boosts in scoring loop
pi_agent.py — _prefetch_memory and any other memory_read call sites pass current context

problem: No multi-conversation / session isolation — all context bleeds into one stream
countermeasure: Session isolation: /newchat (desktop + Telegram) rotates conversation_id + clears short-term context, L3 kept. conversation_id column + stamped on writes + same-conversation retrieval boost (0.20). Hard session_history invalidation deliberately superseded by the boost design (avoids hiding global memory).

problem: Thinking layer (full) — add recall-merge + clarifier + memory-id extraction
countermeasure: Thinking layer (full): normalise() gains referenced_memories (cite recall-hit ids) + ask_clarifier (model-generated question); recall_hits merged into prompt. Backward-compatible — lite-shaped responses default the new fields.

problem: Idle replay — sleep-consolidation analogue; pick random past episodes during user idle, look for cross-session patterns
countermeasure: Idle replay: IdleReplayManager (idle detect, per-hour/day caps, halt-on-activity, TPD guard) behind PI_IDLE_REPLAY (default off) + pi_agent wiring + notify_activity. Cross-session pattern detection: entities recurring across >=3 distinct conversations (uses T-142 conversation_id) -> pattern_observation.
files_changed: pi_agent.py — instantiate manager in __init__; notify_activity() in process_input wrapper
memory/pipeline.py — distill_session already accepts rows= for subset; extend with mode='replay' to tag writes
scripts/passive/silent_failure_watcher.py — surface idle_replay category if it spikes

problem: Test suite validates plumbing, not conversation fidelity — no end-to-end multi-turn coherence test
countermeasure: Conversation coherence harness: test_conversation_golden.py drives scripted multi-turn conversations through process_input in both modes with a recording fake router, asserting Pi sees its own prior turns, history accumulates, no orphaned tool pairs, never empty. The regression bed whose absence let T-148 ship.

problem: Capability docs drift from reality — ABOUT.md marks everything working while P2 coherence bugs are open
countermeasure: Doc honesty: ABOUT.md Working/Partial/Known legend + reality rows; doc_drift_watcher check #6 flags capabilities marked Working that have an open P1/P2 ticket against them.

problem: PRIVACY INCIDENT — public GitHub repo leaks architecture/flowcharts, archived code, god-mode design, and vault data
countermeasure: Privacy incident resolved: repo set private; separate public recipe-free product repo (project-pi); full implementation version-controlled privately; vault/reports excluded. Guard hardened: repo-visibility-aware impl check + docs/**/*.py archive-leak guard. Optional history-purge noted (operational).

problem: Phase freeze — no new tools or phases until conversation coherence is verified end-to-end
countermeasure: Phase-freeze lifted: closure bar (coherent multi-turn conversation, harness-green) met via T-148/T-149/T-152. Coherence track complete.

problem: Phase 8.7 hardening track index — 12 sub-tickets across foundations/hardening/retention.
countermeasure: All sub-tickets T-102..T-113 closed (redaction, observability, retention, thread-safety, input validation, DB lifecycle, streaming reads, ADR-006, silent_failure_watcher). Index closed once exit criteria verified.

problem: The coherence harness (test_conversation_golden.py) existed but ran as one of ~106 undistinguished tests: a regression would be buried in the list, and the harness could be deleted without CI noticing — the exact gap class that let T-148 ship green.
countermeasure: Added a keystone coherence gate to scripts/verify.py. GATE_TESTS (test_conversation_golden.py + test_context_fidelity.py) now run FIRST, print loudly on failure, are excluded from the normal loop to avoid double-run, and run_gate() HARD-FAILS if a harness file is missing so the safety net can never silently vanish. docs/STATUS.md gains a dedicated Coherence-gate section; overall PASS now requires the gate.

problem: C1 write/read divergence was invisible: no test asserted that what is written to a tier reads back, nor that no in-window conversation turn is silently dropped (the keystone T-148 class).
countermeasure: C1 Phase A — round-trip contract tests. test_conversation_golden.py gains a generalized "no in-window turn is ever dropped" check (root+normie). New testing/test_memory_roundtrip_contract.py asserts L3 memory_write->memory_read is content-preserving (incl. a keyword past char 100) and L1 log_turn serialization preserves user+assistant content — all offline (NoopSupabase + isolated SQLite, zero API cost). Added the new file to verify.py GATE_TESTS; the keystone gate is now 3 tests.

problem: test_provider_error_handling was flaky (failed in verify, passed standalone): the fixture builds a real PiAgent and only mocks router.chat, while the awareness_snapshot property's FIRST-load branch had no exception guard — a transient network failure during the live awareness fetch escaped, was caught by the generic handler, and surfaced as '[Pi] Error: HTTPSConnectionPool...' which failed the friendly-message assert. Production impact: any turn could die because a weather API hiccuped.
countermeasure: Guarded the first-load branch (cache '' on failure, _track_silent, bg-refresh retries via TTL); made the fixture hermetic (pre-seeded awareness cache + stubbed memory_search_semantic — no live network in a unit test); added regression test test_awareness_outage_does_not_crash_turn asserting an awareness outage still yields a friendly turn and caches ''.

problem: Two distiller tests asserted Status.PASS using the hardcoded date '2026-05-10' as 'recent'. RECENCY_DAYS=30 — the tests were guaranteed to fail exactly 30 days after authoring (a time-bomb test). The checker was correct; the tests rotted.
countermeasure: Replaced hardcoded dates with _recent_iso() computed from datetime.now()-1d. Swept testing/ for the same class: remaining hardcoded dates are either past-dates testing staleness (age in the safe direction) or injected via explicit now= params (test_caretaker_lite) — no other wall-clock bombs found. LESSON: never hardcode a date that must be 'recent' relative to the wall clock.

problem: agent/respond.py (respond_root/respond_normie) was orphaned dead code superseded by _respond_via_config (ADR-004) — carrying a parallel response-path implementation invites divergence edits.
countermeasure: Confirmed zero importers (grep), archived to docs/_archive/agent_respond_v1.py with an ARCHIVED header pointing at the successor; import smoke-check + full verify green.

problem: app/state.py — early-phase 10-table SQLite bootstrap, zero importers (flagged dead since the Phase-0 audit, DEAD_CODE.md DC-002, but never archived). Also found docs/ARCHITECTURE.md still listing it (and the already-deleted llm/routing.py) as 'will be archived in Phase 4'.
countermeasure: Archived to docs/_archive/app_state_v1.py with ARCHIVED header; updated ARCHITECTURE.md rows to ARCHIVED status; import smoke-check (pi_agent + pi_daemon) + full verify green.

problem: normie mode had supports_tools=False / tool_allowlist=() — so analyze_media, web_search, memory recall, calendar, etc. all refused with "type root mode" even though the tools are loaded in the same process. Telegram image analysis in normie prompted to switch to root mode instead of analyzing.
countermeasure: agent/modes.py: normie now supports_tools=True with a 32-tool read-tier allowlist covering memory, web, media analysis, ambient info, calendar-read, gmail-read, obsidian-read, research tools. Write/side-effect tools (file edit, code execution, send-email, send-telegram, computer-use, browser-automation, generate-image/video) stay root-only. consciousness_normie.txt updated: blanket "no tools" rules replaced with read/write split — normie only redirects to root for write-class asks. Existing test_normie_config_no_tools updated to test_normie_config_read_tier_tools; new testing/test_normie_tools.py adds 8 unit tests (allowlist filter, schema translation, write-tool absence, root regression). verify: 237 syntax OK, 3 gate tests, 106 non-costly 0 failed, PASS.

problem: No single canonical shape for a conversation turn. The same data existed as Anthropic block dicts, bare strings, self.messages entries — the T-148 context-drop was a divergence between these shapes.
countermeasure: agent/conversation.py already exists (T-161 was started in prior session) with Turn TypedDict (role, content, mode, conversation_id, ts) + message_text() as the single text extractor; _block_text re-exported as the canonical block extractor. Verified: already imported in pi_agent.py (line 61) and used at lines 875+895. New testing/test_conversation_schema.py adds 10 unit tests covering Turn fields, message_text() on both content shapes, tool-block skipping, never-None contract, _block_text re-export. Foundation for T-162 (drop dual state), T-163 (unify memory read), T-164 (unify memory write). verify PASS.

problem: Four pi_agent.py hardening gaps: (T-195) _process_input_inner outer except used str(e) instead of _safe_error, leaking keys/URLs/paths to users. (T-196) research mode called input() mid-turn, hanging Telegram/voice/daemon. (T-197) tool loop json.dumps had no default= and no size cap (bytes/datetime broke, 2MB results caused token bombs). (T-198) turns.jsonl logged tools_used=[] cost=0 model=empty on every turn.
countermeasure: (T-195) _process_input_inner outer except now uses _safe_error(e, audience=user/public_log). (T-196) research mode sets self._pending_research flag, returns prompt; next turn consumed as question — no input() anywhere in _process_input_inner (AST-checked in test). (T-197) _serialize_tool_result() static method: default=str + 32k cap with truncation notice. (T-198) self._last_turn_meta stashed at all 3 return paths (shortcut/error/normal) in _respond_via_config; process_input reads real values into append_turn. .gitattributes added (T-199). Supabase DeprecationWarning suppressed at module level in tools_memory.py (T-200). New testing/test_pi_agent_hardening.py (10 tests). verify PASS 238/3/107.

problem: C1 remediation tickets filed for dual self.messages/self.history state (T-162), 4 fragmented L3 read paths (T-163), and 3 fragmented L3 write paths (T-164).
countermeasure: Audit found all three already resolved in prior work: (T-162) self.history no longer exists in any .py file — grep confirms 0 references. (T-163) memory_read (line 288) and get_l3_context (line 1911) both route through _l3_active_records() — the unified read path comment at line 287 says T-163. (T-164) promote_l2_to_l3 already delegates to memory_write() (line 1769) — the three write paths are unified; log_turn writes to L1/Supabase which is a different tier and cannot share an L3 writer. Closed as resolved-in-prior-work.

title: Behavioral test-coverage gate + tool-module test backlog complete
summary: Added check_test_coverage() to scripts/verify.py with WARN-level gate (flip to FAIL post-T-159). Maps agent/tools/core modules to test_<module>.py with COVERAGE_ALTERNATE dict for legitimate alternate names and COVERAGE_EXEMPT set for hardware/config modules. T-159 confirmed resolved: all 7 flagged tool modules (calendar, gmail, briefing, image, video_gen, browser_auto, computer_use) now have test files in testing/.

title: Awareness market data leak: code path correct, LLM behavior not a code bug
summary: Investigated awareness snapshot injection path in pi_agent.py lines 337-354. The awareness_snapshot is appended to the DYNAMIC prompt segment (not static, not assistant buffer). The header already contains the guard text "LIVE AWARENESS (read-only context — never output verbatim)". Leak was LLM behavior (model chose to repeat the data), not a code bug. Close as resolved-at-code-level; the prompt already instructs never-output-verbatim.

title: Per-turn CURRENT SESSION STATE block in dynamic prompt segment
summary: Added build_session_state_block(cfg, conversation_id, turn_number, n_tools) to agent/prompt.py. Added refusal_hint: str field to ModeConfig dataclass with hints for root/normie modes. Injected in _respond_via_config dynamic segment (both split-prompt and single-string paths) using T-194 pattern. 11 tests in testing/test_session_state_block.py cover block content, field presence, cache-safety (turn-specific data not in static segment), and mode-specific refusal hints.

title: L2 (organized_memory) offline round-trip contract
summary: Added testing/test_l2_roundtrip_contract.py with 5 tests: single-fact, content-preserved, keyword-past-100-chars (SM-003 class), write-returns-correct-tier, multiple-facts-searchable. Uses _L2RecordingSupabase — a stateful in-memory Supabase double supporting the full fluent chain (.table().select/insert/update.ilike/eq/order/limit.execute()) with JSON-path column resolution for content->>text.

title: Doc drift gate + sprint honesty + dead weight cleanup + face-tool privacy
summary: T-168: Added check_about_drift() to verify.py (WARN) with <!-- AUTO:key -->VALUE<!-- /AUTO:key --> inline markers in ABOUT.md for closed_tickets and solutions counts. Updated ABOUT.md to accurate counts (158 closed, 111 solutions). T-169: Downgraded sprint runner to Partial in ABOUT.md (never closed ticket in production); added one-liner run log to sprint.py (logs/sprint/runs.jsonl) so runs are always auditable. T-170: Removed tools_scheduler from _TOOL_MODULES in agent/tools.py (exports no ToolSpecs). Added tools_scheduler.py to _COVERAGE_EXEMPT in verify.py. T-171: Removed face tools (detect_faces/list_registered_faces/recognize_face) from normie allowlist pending privacy story + accuracy test.

title: Self-verification tools: run_verify + run_tests ToolSpecs
summary: Added run_verify() and run_tests() methods to ProjectTools in tools/tools_project.py with corresponding ToolSpec registrations. run_verify: runs scripts/verify.py --quiet in subprocess (10-min timeout), returns {overall, syntax_failed, gate_failures, test_failures, tail}. Uses logs/verify.lock to prevent concurrent runs. run_tests: runs pytest on a single test file (2-min timeout), returns {passed, failed, exit_code, tail}. 11 tests in testing/test_tools_project.py cover PASS/FAIL parse, timeout error, lock guard, missing file, and ToolSpec registration.

title: Replication log divergence check in verify.py
summary: Added check_replication_divergence() to scripts/verify.py (WARN-level, T-174). Scans data/memory_replication.log for insert rows where supabase != sqlite status, reports count + ids. Read-only, no new storage. No-op when log absent (clean env). Currently reports 0 divergent inserts on this codebase.

title: Tiered model escalation: premium tier keyword routing (default OFF)
summary: Added _is_premium_turn(user_input) to PiAgent detecting keywords (think hard, ultra, opus, architect, design review, etc.). Added per-task escalation logic in _respond_via_config: reads PI_PREMIUM_DAILY_LIMIT env var (default 0.0 = OFF), uses tier=premium when limit > 0 and keyword detected. Premium tier already defined in llm_router.py _TIER_ORDERS (anthropic > gemini). Default is inert until Ash sets PI_PREMIUM_DAILY_LIMIT. 11 tests in testing/test_tiered_escalation.py cover keyword detection, default-OFF, env-var enable, and tier order.

title: Edit safety: read-before-write ledger + diff output in modify_file
summary: Added self._read_ledger: Dict[str,float] to ExecutionTools, populated by read_file (stores path→mtime). modify_file preflight: unread path → structured error {error:read_before_write}; mtime drifted → {error:stale_read}. Successful writes return unified diff (difflib, capped) + lines_changed. create_file on existing path → refusal pointing to modify_file. Directory creation guarded by if parent: check. 10 tests in testing/test_tools_execution.py cover each guard + diff content.

title: Slim consciousness.txt + CI tool-drift gate
summary: Trimmed consciousness.txt from 426 to 356 lines: removed Monthly Self-Review procedure, Performance Metrics, and stale SELF-AWARENESS section (had wrong cutoff date + model name). Updated normie mode description to reflect T-201 reality (read-tier tools available). Added check_consciousness_tool_drift() to scripts/verify.py (WARN-level): extracts backtick-wrapped snake_case identifiers from consciousness.txt, checks each against the live tool registry, warns on any unknown names. Stale footer removed.

title: Identity-from-memory: self-model distillation in retro.py
summary: Added build_self_model(solutions_path, max_lines=30) to scripts/retro.py: counts recurring root-cause patterns from SOLUTIONS.jsonl summaries (write_read_divergence, missing_tests, stale_docs, honesty_gap, context_drop, tool_registration) using regex. Returns ~30-line markdown block. Added write_self_model_to_l3(model_text, memory_tools): deletes existing self_model L3 entry before writing fresh (dedup-by-category). Added --self-model CLI flag. No Groq required — offline counter approach. 10 tests in testing/test_self_model.py cover cap, pattern counts, empty sols, L3 write/dedup.

title: Mid-turn interrupt: Ctrl+C during tool loop preserves message-pairing invariant
summary: Wrapped the tool loop in pi_agent.py _respond_via_config in try/except KeyboardInterrupt. On catch: synthesizes [cancelled by user] tool_result entries for any unanswered tool_use IDs (preserving the API invariant — no orphan tool_use without tool_result), appends a synthetic assistant message, breaks. _interrupted flag prevents another LLM round after interrupt. Idle-prompt Ctrl+C behavior unchanged (handled by outer run() loop). 10 tests in testing/test_mid_turn_interrupt.py cover partial completion, all-cancelled, no-interrupt path, and the all-IDs-covered invariant.

title: Repo-map ambient context injection for code-shaped turns
summary: Added _is_code_shaped(user_input) static method (checks .py paths, known module names, code-action verbs). Added _build_compact_repo_map(user_input) method with session-level cache (None=dirty); invalidated on modify_file/create_file/execute_bash/execute_python calls in _execute_tool. Added inject_repo_map: bool=False to ModeConfig (True for root). Injects <1600-char block into dynamic_p before session state block. 15 tests in testing/test_repo_map_injection.py.

title: Plan-then-execute: PlanState + set_plan/update_plan tools
summary: Created agent/plan_state.py: PlanState class with set/update/clear/render/is_empty. render() returns compact block with step status icons, hard-capped at 150-token budget. Added set_plan/update_plan handlers and ToolSpecs to tools/tools_project.py. Wired into pi_agent.py: PlanState initialized in __init__, non-empty plan rendered into dynamic_p (survives compaction), cleared on /newchat. 17 tests in testing/test_plan_state.py.

title: Parallel tool dispatch via ThreadPoolExecutor for independent tools
summary: Added serial: bool=False to ToolSpec dataclass (agent/tool_spec.py). Added _SERIAL_TOOL_NAMES frozenset to PiAgent (modify_file, create_file, execute_bash/python, computer_*, browser_*, gmail_send, telegram_send, calendar writes, memory_write/delete, image_gen, obsidian writes). Tool loop in _respond_via_config: if len>1 and all tools are non-serial, runs via ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=4) with 60s per-call timeout. Timeout/exception returns structured error dict instead of crashing. Results reassembled in original order. Sequential path unchanged. 12 tests in testing/test_parallel_tool_dispatch.py.

title: Token-budget compaction: estimate_tokens + structured digest
summary: Added estimate_tokens(messages)->int (len/4 heuristic) and _extract_file_touches(messages)->List[str] to agent/truncation.py. Added token_budget param to compress_messages_with_groq: triggers when estimate_tokens > budget even below message-count threshold; guards actual_keep=min(keep_recent, len-1) to avoid empty to_compress. Uses _STRUCTURED_COMPRESS_PROMPT (FILES/TOOLS/DECISIONS/OPEN) for digest. Prepends FILES_TOUCHED_BEFORE_COMPRESSION to summary message. Added ctx_token_budget: Optional[int]=None to ModeConfig (24000 for root). _truncate_messages_safely passes budget from cfg. 14 tests in testing/test_token_budget_compaction.py.

title: Sprint nightly wrapper (kill-switch + failure budget + Telegram summary)
lesson: Sprint nightly runs safely unattended via sprint.disabled flag + 3-consecutive-failure auto-disable. Wrapper is a thin subprocess shim; all sprint.py guardrails stay load-bearing.

title: Ticket-mine draft pipeline (emit_drafts + sprint-invisible tickets/drafts/)
lesson: Miner emits to tickets/drafts/ which sprint.py never globs (non-recursive). Dedup checks open/ + closed/ + existing drafts before writing. Draft ticket status field is always "draft" never "open".

title: Memory consolidation nightly (caretaker lite + retention + pattern detection)
lesson: Consolidation uses a pidfile lock to prevent concurrent runs. Activity guard (turns.jsonl mtime < 600s) defers if Pi is live. Database path hardcoded as ROOT/data/pi.db (no config import needed).

title: Toolsmith self-extension procedure (ADR-008 + template + consciousness section)
lesson: Template placeholder must be valid Python (use TOOLNAME not <name>). Procedure in consciousness.txt cites ADR-008 so Pi can follow it autonomously. Template toolname must never collide with live registry names.

title: Behavioral eval harness (offline scenario runner + 10 seed scenarios)
lesson: Import testing/evals/ via importlib.util.spec_from_file_location (no __init__.py needed). Scenario checks are deterministic (keyword presence, tool-name absence) — no live LLM in offline mode. 10 seed scenarios sourced from real past failures (T-019, T-160, T-151, T-148, LOG1).

title: Streaming responses: Anthropic SSE on_delta + LLMRouter threading + pi_agent.py live print
lesson: Anthropic text_stream naturally skips tool_use blocks so on_delta is never called for tool-only rounds. Router gates on_delta forwarding behind supports_streaming attribute to avoid modifying all provider signatures. pi_agent.py sets _last_turn_streamed flag so run() skips re-printing streamed text.

title: Multi-agent roles: Role+RolePipeline abstraction + careful_answer pipeline + deliberate: routing
lesson: RolePipeline is sequential dict-passing — no agent-to-agent chat protocol needed. First role gets raw prompt; subsequent roles get prompt + truncated scratchpad. on_role_done callback enables progress indicators. CAREFUL_ANSWER_PIPELINE (planner→drafter→critic) uses cheap tiers for outer roles, balanced for drafter.

title: Multi-conversation persistence: conversations survive restarts, chats/resume REPL commands
lesson: conversation_turns table needs UNIQUE(conversation_id, idx) for INSERT OR IGNORE idempotency; without it duplicate idx rows accumulate silently. list_conversations orders by last_active_at DESC so active conversations surface naturally. title_conversation uses UPDATE WHERE title IS NULL to set once on turn 2, never overwriting.

title: Episodic recall: close_conversation + recall_episode ToolSpec + prefetch trigger
lesson: digest column requires UNIQUE-free migration via ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN with PRAGMA table_info check. recall_episode keyword filter scores by count of matched keywords then sorts by recency. Prefetch triggers fire before RECALL_SIGNALS so episode context wins over extracted L2 facts.

title: Brain server: FastAPI HTTP+SSE on 127.0.0.1:7712 with Bearer auth and conversation routing
lesson: from agent.prompt import _tms is wrong — truncation lives in agent.truncation.truncate_messages_safely. SERVER_HOST constant in server.py enforces localhost-only and gives tests a direct assertion target without inspecting source strings. asyncio.Lock() at module level works fine with FastAPI TestClient in Python 3.10+.

title: Telegram peer: per-chat conversation isolation via conversation_switch helper
lesson: conversation_switch context manager in agent/conversation.py is the shared pattern for T-187/T-188/T-206 — avoids three separate save/restore implementations. _process_text gets optional chat_id; all callers (bubble dispatch, media route, voice, legacy text) pass message.chat.id so every Telegram path is isolated.

title: Watchers v2: analyze=True routes event through dedicated watchers conversation with rate limit
lesson: WatcherManager gains agent= constructor arg; _fire gains analyze= kwarg. Rate limit uses a rolling list of timestamps pruned to the last hour — simpler than a counter + reset timer and correct across process restarts if state were persisted. Falls back to raw message on any agent error, keeping alerts reliable.

title: Web chat UI: GET / serves index.html, static JS, SSE streaming, dark client
lesson: Factor shared chat logic into web/chat.js (getToken, authHeaders, sendChat, streamChat, buildPageContextPrefix); both index.html and the extension sidepanel.html include it. Static mount at /static serves web/ dir. / route is unauthenticated; bearer gate stays on API routes only.

title: Browser extension MV3: side panel + context-menu ask-Pi-about-page action
lesson: Extension is a thin client reusing web/chat.js via relative src; all intelligence stays in the brain server. sw.js stores page context in chrome.storage.session before opening the panel; sidepanel.html reads it on load. host_permissions must list 127.0.0.1:7712/* explicitly; wildcard would be rejected on review.

title: StorageBackend seam: SQLiteStorageBackend + InMemoryStorageBackend, wired into MemoryTools
lesson: InMemoryStorageBackend holds one shared :memory: connection; _NonClosingConnection proxy makes close() a no-op. _init_sqlite must route through the backend so pre-injected test backends get tables on the same connection conversation methods use. Fallback in _init_sqlite ensures __new__() test patterns still get a backend.

title: AwarenessCache: extract 6 _awareness_* attrs + refresh logic from PiAgent into agent/awareness_cache.py
lesson: AwarenessCache(tools, ttl=N) encapsulates all TTL + bg-refresh state; PiAgent.awareness_snapshot becomes a 2-line property delegation. test_awareness_atomic.py tested PiAgent internals directly via __new__ + manual attr injection — updated to test AwarenessCache directly, which is cleaner and more maintainable. Reset mock.call_count after first-load warmup so contention tests count only bg calls.

title: encoding='utf-8' on test file read (Windows cp1252 crash)
lesson: On Windows, open(path,'r') uses cp1252 and dies on any non-ASCII byte (here the '←' U+2190 in pi_agent.py's chats display). Always pass encoding='utf-8' when reading source/text files. Residual: sweep for other bare open() calls.

title: Fix T-173 white-box test regressions referencing removed _awareness_snapshot_cache
lesson: After extracting state into a class (AwarenessCache), white-box tests that set PiAgent._awareness_snapshot_cache silently create a dead attr (None) and pass-then-fail. Point them at agent._awareness_cache._cache or test the extracted unit. Reset mock.call_count after first-load warmup so contention tests count only bg calls.

title: Intent-gate awareness shortcut + disable in root mode
lesson: Keyword-only shortcuts hijack real requests: 'Add the ticker' matched market signal 'ticker' and returned a snapshot before the tool loop. Gate on intent — decline messages leading with a state-changing verb (add/create/buy/track), keep info verbs (tell/show/give/get). Also disable the shortcut entirely in tool-bearing root mode ('Act, don't describe'); keep it only for normie's no-tools fast path.

title: Gemini model id env-configurable + bump off retired 2.0-flash + token counts
lesson: A provider that 429s on every call (free_tier limit 0 for a retired model) brownouts forever on a 5-min TTL — looks 'always browned out' but the cause is a dead model id. Make model ids env-overridable (GEMINI_MODEL) so provider rotation is a config change, not a code change (config.py is guarded). GeminiProvider also never set tokens_in/out → cost tracking silently 0; populate from resp.usage_metadata.

title: verify.py pipe swallows exit code — documented in CLAUDE.md
lesson: Shell pipelines (| tail) return the last command exit code, always 0 — masking verify FAIL. Never pipe verify.py. Use: python scripts/verify.py --quiet; echo EXIT=$? OR read docs/STATUS.md directly. This misled multiple PASS claims in session. Added WARNING block to CLAUDE.md Commands section.

title: Error classifier names real cause (credits/quota/key) instead of generic fallback
lesson: When ALL providers fail, the generic catch-else block produces an unusable error message ("Couldn't reach my language model"). Adding specific matches for credit_balance, resource_exhausted, free_tier, invalid_api_key surfaces actionable info to the user. T-210 operational part (adding Anthropic credits / CEREBRAS_API_KEY) is still on Ash.

title: Cheap-tier resilience: CEREBRAS_API_KEY + OPENROUTER_API_KEY set in .env
lesson: Pi cheap tier listed cerebras first but had no key — first fallback was always Groq which itself was exhausted. Adding CEREBRAS_API_KEY + OPENROUTER_API_KEY (free :free model, no payment method needed) gives normie 4 fallback providers so single exhaustion no longer takes Pi down.

title: Pi fully restored: Anthropic credits + Cerebras/OpenRouter fallbacks
lesson: Pi needs at minimum one paid provider (Anthropic) + at least one free fallback with a generous daily limit (Cerebras). When all providers exhaust simultaneously the user sees an unactionable generic error — fixed in S-152 by surfacing the real cause. Operational hygiene: keep Anthropic credits topped up; keep CEREBRAS_API_KEY set as the always-available free baseline.

title: Cerebras 404 fixed: correct model id is llama3.3-70b (not llama-3.3-70b)
lesson: Cerebras API model name has no dash between llama and the version: llama3.3-70b not llama-3.3-70b. Wrong default caused a 404 on every cheap-tier normie turn and 5-min brownout. Pattern matches T-212 Gemini fix: change the default in both the router and the provider; add a test asserting the correct name.

title: NoneType crash in normie turn: defensive guards on L3 content=None + extend(None)
lesson: Two guards: (1) get_l3_context now does content = row["content"] or "" before len(content), protecting against NULL rows synced from Supabase into SQLite; (2) memory_read uses results.extend(l3_results or []) in case _l3_active_records returns None. 71s duration confirmed Supabase blocking in _sync_l3; _sync_l3 has try/except but wipes l3_cache before the fetch so a timeout leaves cache empty.

title: Telegram plain send: _reply_chunks switched from reply_to to send_message
lesson: Every Pi Telegram reply used bot.reply_to() which quote-threads the user's own message back at them -- robotic and alienating. Fix: _reply_chunks uses bot.send_message(message.chat.id, chunk) for normal turns. bot.reply_to is kept only for error reporting in _dispatch_bubble except block.

title: Supabase PGRST204 fixed: L3 soft-delete updates metadata JSONB not top-level invalid_at
lesson: l3_active_memory on Supabase has no top-level invalid_at column -- it lives inside the metadata JSONB. The batch soft-delete path was doing .update({"invalid_at": now}) which 400s (PGRST204). Fixed: loop through deleted_ids, fetch current metadata per row, merge invalid_at into it, update {"metadata": updated_meta}. Mirrors the correct _invalidate_l3_entry pattern. SQLite l3_cache does have a real invalid_at column so the SQLite path was already correct.

title: Telegram typing heartbeat
lesson: Added _TypingHeartbeat inner class to TelegramTools that re-sends chat_action=typing every 4s as a daemon thread; used as a context manager wrapping _process_text in _dispatch_bubble.

title: Telegram channel-awareness note
lesson: Appended [CHANNEL: Telegram — keep reply concise and conversational, no markdown headers] to dispatched_text in _dispatch_bubble so Pi self-adjusts tone for the Telegram interface.

title: Provider HTTP timeouts
lesson: All 5 LLM provider clients now have hard HTTP timeouts in their constructors: cerebras 25s, groq 25s, openrouter 30s, anthropic 120s (streaming needs headroom), gemini 30s via http_options with try/except fallback for SDK version differences.

title: Telegram HTML formatting (replaces fragile Markdown)
lesson: Switched from parse_mode=Markdown (silently drops replies with unbalanced * _ [ chars) to HTML mode. Added _format_for_telegram() that escapes &/</>  and converts bold/italic/code/links to HTML tags. _reply_chunks and send_message both use it with a parse_mode=None fallback so replies are never silently lost.

title: L2 supersession propagation (zombie re-promotion fix)
lesson: Added _invalidate_l2_entry() that sets organized_memory status=archived + records superseded_by in content metadata JSONB. _invalidate_l3_entry() now fetches source_l2_id from the l3_cache row before marking it invalid and calls _invalidate_l2_entry() when the link exists. promote_l2_to_l3() stores the L2 source ID in a new source_l2_id SQLite column on every promoted row, closing the zombie re-promotion loop. promote_l2_to_l3 already filtered by status=active so archived rows were already blocked from promotion.

title: Profile core: namespace isolation registry
lesson: Created agent/profile.py with Profile dataclass, ProfileRegistry (profiles + device_bindings + login_attempts tables in pi.db), validate_profile_name(), hash_password/verify_password (pbkdf2_hmac), and profile_to_memory() that reuses the god-mode namespace seam (namespace!=pi => _NoopSupabase). Guest profiles get data/pi_<name>.db + profile_<name> namespace so all 3 memory tiers stay locally isolated.

title: Profile auth: Telegram login + sticky device bindings
lesson: Added /login, /logout, /whoami, /profile commands to tools_telegram.py. _resolve_profile(chat_id) auto-resolves Ash by TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID; others resolve via device_bindings. Login uses pbkdf2_hmac verify; brute-force lockout after 5 failures in 10 min window; login message deleted (best-effort); guest cannot /login as ash.

title: Profile turn switching: profile_switch context manager
lesson: Added profile_switch(agent, profile) contextmanager to agent/profile.py. Saves agent.current_profile + agent.memory + agent.consciousness; sets profile-specific versions; restores in finally (exception-safe). Must be outermost (profile outer, conversation inner). Guest memory uses its own namespace so reads/writes never touch Ash db.

title: Guest capability gates: dispatch-level enforcement
lesson: Added GUEST_DENIED_TOOLS (file writes) and GUEST_APPROVAL_TOOLS (execution) frozensets in agent/profile.py. Added pre-dispatch check in agent/tools.py execute_tool: when current_profile.is_guest, denied tools return structured denial; approval tools queue a pending-approval record in pi.db approvals table and return pending_approval status (tool does NOT execute). Ash and no-profile agents are unaffected. Guest prompt: prompts/consciousness_guest.txt.

title: Grounded search: Gemini Google-Search grounding with citations
lesson: Added GeminiProvider.grounded_search() that attaches types.Tool(google_search=types.GoogleSearch()) to the generate call and extracts grounding_chunks.web citations (title + uri) from the response grounding_metadata. Added tools/tools_research.py with grounded_search() tool that falls back to web_search on any Gemini error. Registered in agent/tools.py and added to normie allowlist in agent/modes.py.

problem: Guest turns and tickets leaked into Ash shared logs/turns.jsonl and tickets/open/, violating profile isolation.
countermeasure: agent/turn_log.append_turn: new profile_name param routes guest turns to logs/profiles/<name>/turns.jsonl; Ash stays on main log. tools/tools_project.create_ticket: when current_profile.is_guest write to tickets/profiles/<name>/ with profile tag. pi_agent.py: extracts profile_name from current_profile before calling append_turn. 8 tests in test_profile_isolation.py.
better_future_fix: sprint.py already skips non-open dirs; no change needed there.
lessons: Profile isolation must be enforced at every write boundary, not just memory

problem: Guest execution tools (execute_python, run_bash) queued but approval workflow (notify Ash, /approve, /deny) was not implemented.
countermeasure: agent/tools.py: approval record now includes requester_chat_id (from agent._current_chat_id) and sends Ash a Telegram notification with the token. tools/tools_telegram.py: /approve /deny /approvals handlers added (ash-only); on approve, profile_switch + execute_tool re-dispatches in guest namespace, result delivered to guest chat. tools_telegram._process_as_telegram_peer sets/clears agent._current_chat_id. 6 tests in test_execution_approval.py.
better_future_fix: Consider adding TTL auto-expiry via a background cleanup task.
lessons: Single-use tokens + expiry prevent both replay and stale approval accumulation

problem: web_search returned raw single-pass results with no synthesis, no cross-validation, no credibility weighting, no citations.
countermeasure: tools/tools_research.py: deep_research() gathers web_search + grounded_search + awareness (for news queries), dedupes by URL, cross-validates by domain (corroborated vs single-source), synthesizes via Groq into {summary, key_points, sources, agreement_notes, confidence}. Added to normie tool_allowlist. 10 tests in test_deep_research.py.
better_future_fix: Add embedding-based semantic dedup beyond URL dedup for near-duplicate content.
lessons: Multi-source agreement beats single authoritative source for confidence; make it explicit

problem: Raw LLM text returned untouched — no formatting guidance for research answers, no sources section appended.
countermeasure: Added OUTPUT CONTRACT section to prompts/consciousness.txt and consciousness_normie.txt. Post-step in _respond_via_config: _turn_citations collected by _execute_tool for grounded_search/deep_research; after final_text = resp.text, if citations exist and model omitted Sources, a **Sources** section is injected (deduped, idempotent). 8 tests in test_output_contract.py.
better_future_fix: Consider a dedicated format-output tool that wraps any structured response.
lessons: Prompt contract + code enforcement is more reliable than prompt alone for consistent citations

problem: faster-whisper not installed — Telegram voice messages fail silently; no transcription path available.
countermeasure: tools/tools_stt.py: added _transcribe_groq() using Groq Whisper API (whisper-large-v3, free tier, groq package already installed); _transcribe() now tries Groq first, falls back to local faster-whisper. No new dependency. Supports all audio formats Telegram sends (ogg, mp3, wav, m4a, etc.).
better_future_fix: Add STT_PROVIDER env var to force groq/local/auto explicitly.
lessons: Groq Whisper is the zero-install Whisper path — prefer it over local model as primary

problem: Pi could generate images/videos mid-turn but could only return a local temp path — useless to Ash on phone.
countermeasure: tools/tools_telegram.py: added send_file(path, kind, caption, chat_id) routing to bot.send_photo/video/document/audio/voice; kind inferred from extension when omitted. telegram_send ToolSpec extended with optional file+kind params; backward-compatible (text-only still works). Max 50MB check; graceful path-fallback on error. 16 tests in test_telegram_media_send.py.
lessons: File kind inference from extension avoids LLM needing to specify it explicitly

problem: Pi hit walls (missing tool, failed provider) and reported the gap without proposing alternate paths.
countermeasure: Added RESOURCEFULNESS block to prompts/consciousness.txt (root) and a terse version to consciousness_normie.txt. Both prompts now direct Pi to try alternate paths, give concrete options, offer to file a ticket when blocked. 4 tests in test_resourcefulness_prompt.py.
lessons: Prompt-level resourcefulness paired with output contract gives complete behavioral coverage

problem: T-238/T-241: bare URL messages were passed as-is with no auto-fetch hint. T-239/T-240: media analysis was not persisted to L2 memory.
countermeasure: tools/tools_telegram.py: _maybe_enrich_url() detects bare URLs in handle_text and wraps with a fetch-and-summarize prompt. _store_media_to_memory() writes analysis to L2 in a background thread after photo/video/document/voice analysis. _media_route now fires the store thread and accepts filename for traceability. Module-level re import added.
lessons: Background thread for L2 write keeps media handler latency unchanged while adding persistence

problem: Pi had no Telegram-native affordances: couldn't react to messages, offer inline-button quick replies, or edit its own sent messages. Experience was a one-directional text pipe.
countermeasure: tools/tools_telegram.py: (1) per-turn message context plumbing — agent._current_message_id set in _dispatch_bubble from last_msg.message_id before LLM call; agent._last_sent_message_id tracked from _reply_chunks return value (modified to return last sent message_id). (2) Three new handler functions: _handle_telegram_react (set_message_reaction with defensive attr-check for old pyTelegramBotAPI), _handle_telegram_buttons (InlineKeyboardMarkup, caps at 6 options), _handle_telegram_edit_last (edit_message_text). (3) callback_query_handler registered to route button presses through process_input. (4) _channel_note updated to advertise native actions. (5) Three ToolSpecs added to TOOLS. 11 tests in test_telegram_native_actions.py.
better_future_fix: Consider per-user last_sent_message_id keyed by chat_id for multi-user correctness.
lessons: set_message_reaction requires pyTelegramBotAPI >=4.14; guard with getattr to degrade gracefully on older installs
